author: omerfirmak
content:
there seems to be a slight difference between the EIP and spec. 

spec (https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/blob/eips/amsterdam/eip-7928/src/ethereum/forks/amsterdam/vm/instructions/environment.py#L397-L402) charges the whole total cost of the opcode (`access_gas_cost + copy_gas_cost + extend_memory.cost`) before accessing the target address, but EIP (https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/blob/master/EIPS/eip-7928.md?plain=1#L147) requires only `access_cost + memory_expansion` to be charged before the state access.

author: nero_eth
content:
You're right and I clarified/fixed it [here](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11718).

Tests and specs had it already correct, so, clients passing the `test_bal_extcodecopy_and_oog::oog_at_memory_boundary` test are fine.

author: holgerd77
content:
We are now ready with our EthereumJS BAL implementation, passing all tests from v7.1.0.

These are the identified test gaps after implementation. <@547171287831805972> let me know if you identify some of these as legit, I could eventually help with test creation on some of them.

**EIP-7928 BAL — suggested test additions**

**1. CREATE2 early failure** (insufficient endowment)
Same bug class as CREATE: the computed address must **not** enter the BAL on early failure. Only CREATE is covered today.

**2. Cross-tx storage no-op preserves prior change**Tx2 re-writing the **same** value must not remove tx1’s `storage_changes`. Single-tx no-op is covered; cross-tx zeroing is covered — this middle case is not.

**3. BAL gas limit after post-tx system work**Item count must be checked on the **final** BAL (incl. EIP-7002/7251), not right after txs. Current test uses an empty block only.

**4a. 7702 delegation blocked by insufficient CALL value**
Updated spec: delegated address stays out of BAL if load gates fail. No dedicated test yet; may conflict with existing insufficient-funds fixture.

**4b. 7702 delegation blocked by stack depth**
Same gate family as 4a — stack at limit before delegation load.

author: gurukamath
content: [PR#2882](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2882) and [PR#2473](https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/pull/2473) should already handle 4a iirc. As far as 4b is concerned, the stack depth limit cannot be triggered with the current tx gas limit

author: nero_eth
content: Let's use this thread to discuss this further. Would be great to get input from all clients (how they're handling it today and what they'd prefer).
